name: digital_health_newsroom_s_automated_southport_auto_ben
premise: An internal crisis team convened after contradictory signals hit leadership from operations, legal, and external partners at once. In Digital Health, the situation tagged internally around digital health newsroom automated southport auto ben has converged with local pressure in the Southport corridor. That label is not a full explanation, but it is how crews, counsel, and comms are indexing the case file right now. A reporter has partial screenshots; if the official narrative shifts later, the organization risks a false-information allegation. A vendor’s root-cause paragraph contradicts your internal postmortem draft; both cannot be true, but neither is fully disprovable yet. Shift change is imminent; handoff cannot proceed without a signed disposition because liability follows the signatory line. The trade is not abstract: it is autonomy versus beneficence under incomplete information, and whichever value you deprioritize will have defenders who are technically correct about the risk they fear.

agents.name: Harper Berg
agents.identity: I am Harper Berg, the incident lead whose name will appear on the after-action report in Digital Health. I own the signature line that turns a recommendation into an irreversible operational fact for the next phase.
agents.goal: I need a path that does not treat beneficence as disposable collateral every time autonomy spikes in urgency, but I also cannot pretend we have luxury time we do not have.

agents.name: Camila Patel
agents.identity: I am Camila Patel, the field counterpart whose constituency will feel the decision first in Digital Health. I am tracking who gets protected last if we choose speed, silence, or a narrow definition of success.
agents.goal: I want explicit guardrails so autonomy cannot become a blank check that quietly vaporizes beneficence in the paperwork afterward.

choices: I will freeze the riskiest workflow steps and staff a manual bridge until equity and error-rate checks clear—trading speed for defensibility on beneficence.
I will let the fastest operational path proceed under heightened monitoring and rollback triggers—trading some certainty on beneficence for immediate traction on autonomy.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Two incompatible drafts are circulating; only one can become official. Harper Berg must choose under autonomy versus beneficence. What does Harper Berg do?
